message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
*Incident Number INC100087:* test summary
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@manuj.grover-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Seems to not work for me as well, could see some 504s and 404s in the grafana dashboard
Can someone guage the impact for the same? Is this happening for specific region or it's a global issue
:star: Reproduces the incident
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
We are seeing some issues with checkout page, and the team is currently guaging the impact.
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
The spike in failed transactions is mostly from UK users, so the impact appears regional rather than global.
:star: Qualify the problem
I need specifics. Which regions are affected, and how many customers are unable to complete purchases? Are we currently losing revenue?
I'm starting an incident to get folks on board and start fixing the issue
*Incident Number INC100034:* sev1 checkout page breaking
Payment service health checks are returning 504s and latency has increased significantly, which aligns with the reported failures.
504 errors from the payment service are causing transaction failures, and these errors started appearing after the recent deployment.
The latest diagonsis suggests it's limited to some parts of europe only
Did we made any recent changes in payment service?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Could this be related to any payment aggregator as well? let's look those once as well
Any updates on the investigation?
The 504 errors are consistent for UK transaction IDs and began right after the latest deployment, which suggests the issue may be upstream of our payment service or related to a recent change.
Given the timing and regional pattern, it's possible the integration with the UK payment aggregator is affected, so we should review any recent changes to that code path.
Understood. I need exact numbers—how many customers or orders are impacted in those European regions? Are we seeing a measurable drop in revenue right now?
Could see there is this change: `CHG89362` done in payment service in last 20 mins, and the issue seems to be appearing from around 10 mins
My change (CHG89362) is for Bitcoin payments and the new code isn't being called by the frontend yet, so it's highly unlikely to be the cause.
Have you checked if Shay's checkout service change (CHG89360) could be causing the issue, since it went live an hour ago?
Can we revert this change?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
Cool makes sense, let's revert those changes first
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
If that doesn't help, let's quickly investage payments
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
Let's revert payment service changes as well then
I've started digging into the payment service changes and will report back with anything unusual I find.
Just to reiterate, my code was thoroughly tested and shouldn't affect GBP payments, but I'm open to reviewing the logs if needed.
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
Please confirm if we have reverted these changes: `CHG89360` and `CHG89361`. If not please do them asap.
:star: Manage actions and timings
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
Do we have any rough numbers on how many customer could be impacted from this?
I don't have exact figures, but based on the UK traffic volume, this could be affecting a significant portion of our customers in that region.
Bob, can we check if the payment service is working as expected? and if uk customers payments have started recovering
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
From the latest updates, feels like major portion of UK customers were impacted
I need concrete figures. How many UK customers are affected? What is the estimated revenue loss so far? Provide a timeline for resolution.
Will update in sometime, we are currently minimising the impact
Grafana metrics confirms it, but please keep a close look and check for other anomalyies
Glad to hear metrics are recovering. Would you like me to check the service logs for any lingering errors or anomalies?
Standing by to run a test transaction if you want to double-check the internal flow.
yes, check for test transaction and workflows
I'll check the payment service logs now.
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
We have confirmed the issue is now resolved.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Has order flow and revenue fully recovered? Please confirm that transactions are processing normally for all UK customers.
Thank you team for promptly solving this!

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/7996/feedback
